name: vitals-trend-chart
title: Vitals Trend Chart
description: Vitals over time. Series that do not share a unit are drawn as separate panels over a common x axis — never as two y-scales on one plot.

props.name: data
props.description: Rows keyed by `xKey` plus each series key. `null` means not measured.

props.name: series
props.description: `{ key, label, unit, color?, domain?, range? }`. Series are grouped into panels by `unit`.

props.name: label
props.description: Required. Names the figure for assistive tech.

props.name: events
props.description: Clinical events aligned to the x axis — a dose, a position change, an escalation.

clinicalNotes: The component refuses a dual axis. A second y-scale silently rescales one series against another — it is how a respiratory rate of 20 gets read against a heart rate of 20 — so series with different units become separate stacked panels sharing one time axis, and every value stays on the only scale it means anything in. Missing observations are not bridged: a line drawn across a gap is a measurement nobody took. Ranges are per-series and caller-supplied, because a normal heart rate for a neonate is a tachycardia in an adult. Events are markers only; their alignment with a change is not a causal claim.
notes: Composes chart + reference-range-band. Each panel renders at h-48.
